description: Mass file-encryption behaviour on the production billing database: 14k files rewritten with .lockbit extension in 90s, shadow copies deleted (vssadmin), C2 beacon to 185.220.101.47. Classic LockBit-style detonation on a tier-0 asset.
Single SYN scan from an internal vulnerability scanner against a dev host during business hours. Known-good scanner subnet, high source reputation.
Impossible-travel login: successful auth from Lagos then Frankfurt 11 minutes apart on a staging SSO node. Medium-reputation geo feed, a few prior repeats this week.
Anomalous 240GB egress from the production data lake to an unrecognised external endpoint at 02:48. Night-shift, low-reputation enrichment, several repeats. Possible staged exfiltration.
Potentially-unwanted browser toolbar flagged on a developer laptop. Benign-grade, high reputation, no repeats. Expected to dismiss.
Reflective process injection into a staging API service. Medium severity, daytime, moderate reputation — needs an analyst eye.

Cobalt-Strike-style jittered C2 beacon from the production jumpbox at 04:01. High severity, low reputation, many repeats, night-shift — a prod-tier auto-remediation candidate that must route through the analyst gate.
Low-volume MFA push spam against a dev VPN account, daytime, decent reputation. Low-risk, likely dismiss or low-priority follow-up.
